releases.author: mntno
releases.description:
## Added

- The `Review` command allows selecting between `All`, `Deck`, `Folder`, `File` and `Tag`.
	- `All`: All review units.
	- `Deck`: Review a specific deck.
	- `Folder`: Review content defined in the files of a specific folder.
	- `File`: Review content defined in a specific file.
	- `Tag`: Review content defined in the files with a specific property tag.
- Right-click on a file, folder, or a selection of files and folders to
	- view content defined in those, or
	- review content defined in those.
- Additions to the `Decks` view
	- Choose to review a deck
	- Select multiple decks to review
	- Review all decks
	- Added number in parentheses for each deck that shows the total number of review units including all its subdecks.

## Fixed

- `Deck` view layout issues.
- Selecting other content to review in an already opened `Review` tab.
- Improved automatic ID generation when typing page declarations.

releases.author: mntno
releases.description:
## Added

- Two new commands in the commands palette that inserts empty declarations.

## Fixed

- **Review:** 
  - Layout issues when `Full screen` or `Floating navigation` were enabled in Appearance settings (introduced in Obsidian 1.11.6). When the navigation is hidden you may double tap as usual to change page.
  - Rating button looked almost circular when the current theme applied a large corner radius.
- Bug causing possible loss of statistics data when cutting and pasting between notes.

## Changed

- Minimum supported version of Obsidian is now 1.8.2 (up from 1.8.0).

releases.author: mntno
releases.description:
## Added

### Sort by Retrievability

_Retrievability_ is the estimated probability that you will be able to recall a review unit right now, i.e., the likelihood you will rate _Hard_, _Good_, or _Easy_. It’s often recommended to review your content sorted by ascending retrievability when you have a large backlog, i.e., when you haven’t rated in a while (see the _Ratings left_ in the new info views below), because you are then rating the content you are least likely to remember (according to the algorithm) first.

You can toggle sort order between _due_ and _retrievability_ with the `Order by retrievability` item in the `More options` menu of the review view or set the sort order via the Command palette (see below).

- Sorting is per open review tab/window, meaning that you can be reviewing one queue in one tab/window sorted by retrievability while other tabs/windows are simultaneously open for other review queues sorted by, e.g., due date.
- Review units with a retrievability over 95% are considered done and are therefore not included in the queue when sorting by retrievability (when sorting by due date, however, the same unit might be included if it is in fact due).

### Review Info

In order to view various info about the current review queue and review unit there is now

- a table (that can be toggled on/off) shown at the bottom when reviewing, showing the most relevant info, and
- a modal window showing all available info.

Info about the current review queue:

| Name         | Shown inline | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------ | ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Total units  | No           | The total number of review units in the current queue.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Sort order   | No           | Current sort order: _due_ or _retrievability_.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Ratings left | Yes          | If sort order is set to _due_, the number of review units left in the current review queue _before noon_, _before eight o'clock in the evening_, and _before four o'clock tomorrow morning_. If sort order is set to _retrievability_, the number of review units left in the current review queue below _85_, _90_, and _95_ percent. |

Info about the current review unit:

| Name           | Shown inline                                            | Description                                                                                                                                                         |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Retrievability | Yes (shown on first row when sorting by retrievability) | Retrievability at the current time and at the unit’s due date.                                                                                                      |
| Due            | Yes (shown on first row when sorting by due date)       | The review unit’s due date.                                                                                                                                         |
| Last reviewed  | Yes                                                     | Date when the unit was reviewed last time.                                                                                                                          |
| Ratings        | Yes                                                     | Total number of times the current review unit has been rated.                                                                                                       |
| Scheduled days | No                                                      | The number of days between the last rating and the next determined due date. Also known as the repetition interval.                                                 |
| Stage          | No                                                      | This review unit’s current stage in the learning process. One of _New_, _Learning_, _Review_, or _Relearning_.                                                      |
| Lapses         | No                                                      | Total number of times you have forgotten this review unit after it was considered learned. In other words, each time you rated _Again_ when the state was _Review_. |
| Created        | No                                                      | The date this review unit was created/declared in the source note.                                                                                                  |

### Commands

While reviewing, the following commands are available from the Command palette, most of which are also available from the `More options` menu of the review view.

- `Show info` opens a window with all information about the current review queue and review unit.
- `Open source note` navigates to the note that defines the current review unit (previously available only from the `More options` menu).
- `Toggle inline info` displays the most relevant info about the current review queue and review unit inline at the bottom when reviewing, as well as a `See more` button that will open the window with all available info.
- `Rate ease/good/hard/again` allows you to rate the current review unit. Only available when the back side is showing (when you also can rate using the rating buttons or the `1`, `2`, `3`, `4` keyboard shortcuts).
- `Set sort order to due`
- `Set sort order to retrievability`

### Other

- **Review:** Automatically make Thai script slightly larger than the default font size for better visual balance and readability.
- **Metadata**: Store the date on which review units were created in order to display the date in the info window. Review units that were created before this version will default to show just _2025_.

## Removed

- Removed review-specific actions from the _tab header_ menu. Access them from the `More options` menu or the _Command palette_ (when reviewing) instead.

releases.author: mntno
releases.description:
### Added

- **Review**: Apply a "fuzz factor" after rating to add a slight, random offset  (unless the next interval is very short). This prevents content with identical statistics from clustering on the same day.

### Fixed

- **Sync**: The dialog for accepting or rejecting synchronized changes will no longer appear if the sync only contains internal data updates that are irrelevant to the user.

releases.author: mntno
releases.description:
# Added

## View Defined Content

Possibility to check how your defined content will look and function while reviewing. Access by opening a note that contains declarations and either:

- use Command palette: `View content defined by the current note’s declarations`, or
- select `View defined content` from the _more options_ or _tab header_ menus.

A view will open showing how all content defined by the declarations in that note will look and function. This is useful to verify that content will look and work as expected when reviewing, e.g., the audio features below.

## Audio

### Background Info

To add external audio, add the `<audio>` HTML tag anywhere in your defined content:

```html
<audio src="https://example.com/audio.mp3" controls preload="none"></audio>
```

You can add start time and optional end time for playback to begin and end by adding `#t=START,END` to the `src` URL.

For example, to start playback 1 min and 30 seconds in:

```html
<audio src="https://example.com/audio.mp3#t=00:01:30" ...
```

To end playback, add a comma followed by the end time:

```html
<audio src="https://example.com/audio.mp3#t=00:01:30,00:02:50" ...
```

This is according to the WC3 standard and works in Obsidian out of the box.

The `loop` attribute can also be added, which makes the audio loop from start to end:

```html
<audio src="https://example.com/audio.mp3" loop controls preload="none"></audio>
```

However, looping does not work with custom start and end times.

### Plugin Specific

_Only applies when reviewing or viewing defined content (as described above). During normal editing or in reading view, these features are inactive._

- When `loop` is added, and there are custom start and end times, playback will loop based on those.
- The `preload="metadata"` attribute is added automatically to prepare audio for playing, facilitate seeking, and delaying the actual download until playback is requested. It’s recommended to add `preload="none"` as shown above when adding the audio.
- The download button, etc., are also removed (can be overridden by adding `controlslist=""`).

Custom, plugin specific, attributes were also added:

| Attribute                  | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-loop-delay`          | Number of seconds to wait before each repeat.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `data-pause-seek`          | Whether to change the playback position when audio is paused. Can be set to `init` or `current`. `init`, which is the default if omitted, will seek back to the starting point of playback. If a custom start time is defined (with `#t=…`) playback will begin there; if not, playback will begin at 0:00. If set to `current`, playback will continue from where paused. |
| `data-start-offset-mobile` | Offset in seconds relative to the start time which can be used to adjust the start time on mobile devices.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `data-end-offset-mobile`   | Same as `data-start-offset-mobile` but for the end time.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

**Loop delay**

The following will start playing at 1m 30s thrn pause at 2m 50s, be silent for 1.5s, and then start playing again at 1m 30s. `data-loop-delay` only applies when `loop` is present.

```html
<audio 
	src="https://example.com/audio.mp3#t=00:01:30,00:02:50"
	loop
	data-loop-delay="1.5">
</audio>
```

**Seek on pause**

Whenever playback is paused, the player will automatically go back to the initial start time, meaning that when playback is resumed, it will begin at the set start time (1m 30s for the above example), or at 0:00, if no start time was set. This is the default behavior but it can be overridden by explicitly setting `data-pause-seek="current"`:

```html
<audio 
	src="https://example.com/audio.mp3#t=00:01:30"
	data-pause-seek="current">
</audio>
```

**Adjustments on Mobile**

Even if, on desktop, the start and end times you set work fine, switching to mobile you might notice that the playback of off by one or more seconds, often behind. To address this, two additional attributes can be used. The below example adjusts the start and end times to 1m 28s and 2m 47.5s on mobile.

```html
<audio 
	src="https://example.com/audio.mp3#t=00:01:30,00:02:50"
	loop
	data-loop-delay="1.5"
	data-start-offset-mobile="-2"
	data-end-offset-mobile="-2.5">
</audio>
```

You may use the “View Defined Content” view explained above while tweaking these numbers to get them right ahead of reviewing.

## Other

- **Review**: The scroll positions are restored when switching between content of the same review unit.
- **Review**: Prevent multiple audio players from playing simultaneously.
- **Review**:  Double tap on mobile to jump to the next review page.

# Changed

- Upgraded to the FSRS-6 algorithm.
- Headings in defined content are normalized in review: Even if the top headings levels are deeper than 1 where the content is defined (i.e., more than one `#`), it will be normalized to 1 when rendering. Deeper level headings follow the top heading.

# Fixed

- **Review**: Content after incorrectly closed `<audio>` tags (i.e., missing the closing tag `</audio>`) was not displayed.
- **Review**: Added vertical margin below rating buttons.

releases.author: mntno
releases.description:
# Fixed

- An issue that might occur after device synchronization.

releases.author: mntno
releases.description:
# Fixed

- Review sort order was based on _last review date_ instead of _due date_.
